Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 15:01 ET
Institutional dispersion hedge accelerates as single-name calls clash with index puts
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. The P/C divergence has reached extreme levels — index puts at 1.50 while single-names see calls at 0.86, signaling institutional dispersion hedging ahead of NFP.
  • 2. Negative gamma at -$18.4B amplifies the AMD block trade significance, with 40,000 contracts betting on post-earnings momentum through June.
  • 3. Vol surface contradictions abound — nine names in backwardation while correlation sits at 0.30, creating tactical dispersion opportunities.
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedge acceleration

The most striking development is the extreme P/C divergence reaching institutional hedge fund levels, with index puts at 1.50 ratio while single-name calls dominate at 0.86 — a 0.64 spread that screams dispersion positioning ahead of Friday's NFP print. This structure typically emerges when sophisticated money expects stock-specific outperformance within a macro hedge, supported by implied correlation collapsing to 0.30 in a clear stock-specific regime. The timing into NFP suggests funds are positioned for individual earnings beats while maintaining index downside protection. AMD's massive call positioning stands out against this backdrop, with 40,000 contracts across May 185C and June 180C strikes totaling $62.68 in premium — a $250M+ bet on post-earnings momentum that gains explosive potential in the current -$18.4B negative gamma environment. The technical golden cross at 77.2 score conflicts with the death cross signal, but the RSI at 62 with MACD positive suggests the options flow is betting on resolution higher. Energy's XLE seeing both technical strength (91.5 score, RSI 83) and large put blocks creates an interesting cross-current, with 21,500 XLE puts potentially signaling profit-taking on the sector's outperformance. The vol surface reveals tactical opportunities in the chaos, with nine names showing backwardation (elevated near-term fear) while correlation remains suppressed — classic conditions for long single-name vol versus short index vol trades. Names like DASH showing +141.7% IV rich versus sector while COST trades -36.1% cheap create clear relative value plays, particularly when combined with the broader dispersion theme dominating institutional flow.

Layer 1: Sentiment -35Layer 2: GEX -$18.4BLayer 3: P/C divergence 0.64Layer 4: Correlation 0.30
